codeReview.summary: The feature achieves VAL-ASYNC-003: the timelock retains EMERGENCY access, the residual 0x5198 holder was removed on-chain, the proof artifact captures pre/post hasRole state, and the canonical manifest plus README were updated. I found no blocking defects, but there are two non-blocking maintainability/auditability issues.

issues.description: If the transfer has already been completed, the script returns before writing the proof artifact, so rerunning it cannot regenerate audit evidence from an already-correct on-chain state.

The manifest was manually updated to point roles.emergencyAdmin at the timelock, but generatedAt still reports the older 2026-03-16 timestamp, leaving the canonical manifest metadata stale after the March 19 role transfer.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission feature spec is now stale about the transfer mechanics: features.json still says this was a 2-TX grantRole + revokeRole operation, but the completed live run was a 1-TX renounceRole cleanup because the timelock already held EMERGENCY.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/features.json:1864 says 'via AccessManager.grantRole + revokeRole' and 'This is a 2-TX admin operation'; /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md:111-116 and the handoff show the actual fix was accessManager.renounceRole(1, 0x5198...) because the timelock already had the role.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is an undocumented deployment gotcha around role holders: the project supports a distinct EMERGENCY_PRIVATE_KEY actor, but the governed deployment cleanup only revokes EMERGENCY from the deployer signer. That mismatch caused the residual emergency holder this feature had to clean up, and the caveat is not captured in .factory/library/environment.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/.env.example:27-31 defines separate ADMIN/EMERGENCY/RISK/TREASURY/MINTER private keys; /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts:54-55 and 119 grant EMERGENCY to emergencyAdmin; /home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deployGovernedSystem.ts:159-160 revokes EMERGENCY only from deployerAddr; /home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/environment.md documents role-specific keys generically but not this cleanup hazard.

summary: Pass. The live admin transfer is evidenced by commit 58d8bb2, proof artifact data, README/manifest updates, and shared-state AGENTS notes, and it satisfies the required hasRole end state. I found no blocking problems; only rerun auditability and stale manifest metadata need follow-up, plus two shared-state knowledge gaps for future workers.
